name: San Francisco (United States)

colors.symbolism: the city's official colour ("Oro en Paz" — "Gold in Peace")
the rising flames around the phoenix
the lettering of the city's name

description: A white field bearing in the centre a phoenix rising from flames in red and gold; below the phoenix on a gold scroll the motto "ORO EN PAZ, FIERRO EN GUERRA" ("Gold in Peace, Iron in War"); along the lower edge in bold blue letters, the city name "SAN FRANCISCO". The flag is bordered top and bottom by a thin gold band.
history: The original design was executed in 1900 by sculptor Robert Ingersoll Aitken — although the phoenix had been a civic symbol of San Francisco at least since 1852, when it featured on the city's first official seal. The Board of Supervisors codified the present design — adding the city name in bold blue letters along the bottom — on 16 December 1940. The phoenix is widely (though not exclusively) associated with the city's recovery from the 1906 earthquake and fires.

features.symbolism: plain white field, framed top and bottom by a thin gold band

the phoenix has been a civic symbol of San Francisco at least since 1852, when it featured on the first official city seal; it is now strongly associated with the city's rebirth from the 1906 earthquake and fires
